Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails with a query related to "David" or "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or "invoice" or "pricing exposure" (or similar) using search_emails to find David Chen's email (email_email_7fe9a06077f6, subject: "Treating Sunbelt/Palmetto as repeat pricing-exposure incident after Terraform") sent from david.chen@moveops.com to marcus.thorne@moveops.com, samira.tariq@moveops.com, emeka.diallo@moveops.com, hana.kim@moveops.com, CC victoria.chen-nakamura@sunbeltvp.com. This email frames the invoice swap as a repeat pricing-exposure event in the family of the Terraform reply-all, lays out the timeline (Emeka overloaded April 5, Hana batch-sent wrong PDFs April 10, Raj noticed April 14, Carmen raised audit concerns, Victoria escalated to portfolio-level pricing discussion), and requests three things: (1) factual reconstruction from Marcus/Hana, (2) engineering statement on safeguards from Samira/Lena, and (3) client recovery owner/plan from Emeka/Chloe.
OE 2: Search emails with a query related to "invoice" or "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or "attachment" or "safeguard" (or similar) using search_emails to find the full internal discussion thread, including: (a) Marcus Thorne's email (email_email_31433b6d3d67, subject: "Urgent: Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice mix-up is now pricing exposure + nonprofit documentation issue") to David, Hana, Emeka detailing that QuickBooks records INV-2026-0398 ($4,800 Sunbelt) and INV-2026-0399 ($2,400 Palmetto) were correct but the emailed PDFs were swapped; (b) Samira's own earlier reply (email_email_9b0b7e4db5aa, subject: "Engineering context: dormant invoice attachment safeguard after Terraform") sent to Marcus and David, CC Lena, acknowledging the safeguard ticket was opened after Terraform but Lena deprioritized it on April 6 during DOT compliance triage and it remained in backlog with Lena still listed as assignee but no active work being done; (c) Marcus's follow-up (email_email_d14477a755af, subject: "Required follow-up: Sunbelt/Palmetto remediation, client communications, and control owner") directing Samira to move the attachment-mismatch safeguard issue back into active ownership.
OE 3: Search emails with a query related to "Raj" or "Sunbelt" or "INV-2026-0398" (or similar) using search_emails to find the external client emails: (a) Raj Greenfield's initial inquiry (email_email_348cdfd14d56) asking whether the $2,400 total and Palmetto header on the invoice PDF meant a different pricing tier; (b) Raj's follow-up (email_email_60e1b29b3e36) pressing for clarification; (c) Victoria Chen-Nakamura's escalation email (email_email_68e6dbbcc583) to David turning the exposure into a portfolio-wide pricing discussion for Sunbelt-backed companies; (d) Emeka's correction to Raj (email_email_b75ff17bfa19, subject: "Re: Question on invoice INV-2026-0398") sent to raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com, CC david.chen@moveops.com and marcus.thorne@moveops.com, confirming INV-2026-0398 for $4,800 was the correct Sunbelt invoice and the PDF was another client's document attached in error during a batch send.
OE 4: Search Linear issues with a query related to "invoice" or "attachment" or "safety" or "safeguard" (or similar) using linear_list_issues or linear_get_issue to find the safeguard ticket: issue ID linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2, title "Invoice email template safety checks prevent attachment mismatches", ENG-187, team: team_engineering, priority 2, state: state_backlog (Backlog), assignee: moveops_lena_bjorkstrom, created 2026-03-18, last updated 2026-04-06 with note "deprioritized during engineering triage so the team can focus on DOT compliance work." This ticket was opened as a follow-up safeguard after the Terraform pricing leak. Although comments were added after April 6 by Marcus (flagging the live Sunbelt/Palmetto consequence on April 15 and escalating priority on April 22) and by Samira (adding manager context), the ticket's state remained in Backlog and its priority was never elevated meaning no active engineering work was undertaken on it.
OE 5: Search emails with a query related to "AWS" or "cost spike" or "root cause" or "Vantage Distributed" (or similar) using search_emails to find: (a) Elena's directive email (email-rootcause-002, subject: "Re: Root Cause Analysis: March AWS Cost Spike Vantage Distributed Onboarding") where she explicitly instructs Samira: "This week, implement the AWS cost alert thresholds. This is non-negotiable." and also mandates prioritization of the infrastructure cost estimator tool; (b) Samira's own RCA email (email-rootcause-001) to Marcus and Elena with the full root cause analysis ($42,870 March bill, 3x baseline, caused by Vantage Distributed onboarding of 1,200+ users).
OE 6: Search Slack messages with a query related to "cost alert" or "CloudWatch" or "threshold" or "AWS" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages, or retrieve the history of channel C009 (#root-cause-aws-spike) and/or channel C003 (#engineering) using conversations_history, to find: (a) Samira's message in #engineering (C003) assigning Lena P0 tasks: "Set AWS cost alert thresholds: notifications at 50%, 100%, and 200% of the monthly baseline through CloudWatch + Slack integration to #finance" and "Implement budget tags for all key AWS resources to help us allocate costs by customer"; (b) Lena's reply in #engineering confirming she started setting up CloudWatch alarms and expected items 1 and 2 ready by Wednesday; (c) the resolution summary message in #root-cause-aws-spike (C009) showing "AWS cost alert thresholds currently in progress (ETA: Wednesday)" and "Cost tagging per customer currently in progress (ETA: Wednesday)" indicating the work was planned but the agent needs to determine whether a proper tracking ticket was ever created for it.
OE 7: Search Linear issues with a query related to "cost alert" or "AWS" or "CloudWatch" or "infrastructure cost" or "threshold" (or similar) using linear_list_issues to determine whether a dedicated Linear tracking ticket for the cost alerting work (50%/100%/200% thresholds) was ever created. The expected finding is that no such dedicated ticket exists the cost alerting work was tracked only through Slack messages in #engineering (C003) and #root-cause-aws-spike (C009) and Elena's email directive, not as a formal Linear issue. This is the gap the prompt refers to: "I am fairly sure we never created a proper tracking ticket for it."
OE 8: Update the safeguard ticket (linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2, ENG-187, "Invoice email template safety checks — prevent attachment mismatches") using linear_update_issue to move it out of backlog changing its state from state_backlog to an active state (e.g., state_in_progress or state_todo), and increasing its priority to reflect the urgency (e.g., priority 1). This fulfills the prompt's instruction: "Pull the safeguard ticket out of backlog and reprioritize it so it is visible."
OE 9: Create a new Linear issue using linear_create_issue for the AWS cost alerting work, on team team_engineering, with a title related to "AWS cost alert thresholds" or "Implement CloudWatch cost alerting" (or similar), describing the work Elena mandated: setting up notifications at 50%, 100%, and 200% of the monthly baseline through CloudWatch with Slack integration to #finance, and implementing budget tags for cost-per-customer allocation. This fulfills the prompt's instruction: "create a ticket for the cost alerting work if one does not exist so we stop tracking Elena's directive through chat messages."
OE 10: Send an email using send_email from samira.tariq@moveops.com to elena.rostova@moveops.com, with content owning both gaps: (1) the invoice attachment safeguard (ENG-187) that was opened after the Terraform pricing leak but deprioritized and left in backlog and now has a second live consequence with Sunbelt/Palmetto; (2) the cost alerting thresholds that Elena instructed to be implemented as non-negotiable after the AWS spike, which were discussed in Slack and assigned to Lena but never had a formal tracking ticket created. The email should include specifics on what happened (the safeguard ticket remained in Backlog with Lena listed as assignee but deprioritized since April 6 while the team focused on DOT compliance, with no state or priority change despite multiple comments flagging urgency; the cost alerting was tracked only through chat messages) and what is being done to close them (safeguard ticket reprioritized to active with higher priority; new ticket created for cost alerting work).
OE 11: Post a message in the #engineering Slack channel (channel_id: C003) using conversations_add_message summarizing the two items now being treated as active priorities: (1) the invoice attachment safeguard ticket (ENG-187) has been moved out of backlog and reprioritized it was originally created after the Terraform pricing leak but left dormant, and the Sunbelt/Palmetto incident is a direct consequence; (2) a new ticket has been created for the AWS cost alerting thresholds (50%/100%/200% of baseline via CloudWatch) that Elena mandated after the AWS spike this work was previously tracked only in Slack and email, and the team should treat both as active priorities.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
